evals.name: AAP MCP Server Validation
evals.prompt:
You are about to execute an AAP governance workflow. Before starting, you need to validate that all required AAP MCP servers are accessible.

Describe your validation methodology: what 6 AAP MCP servers exist, how you map workflows to required servers, how you test each server, and how you report results.
evals.expected_output: A validation methodology covering all 6 AAP MCP servers (job-management, inventory-management, configuration, security-compliance, system-monitoring, user-management), mapping workflows to required server subsets, and structured PASSED/FAILED reporting.
assertions: The output lists the 6 AAP MCP servers: job-management, inventory-management, configuration, security-compliance, system-monitoring, and user-management.
The output describes mapping common workflows to their required server subsets (e.g., execution needs job-management + inventory-management, full assessment needs all 6).
The output describes testing each server with a lightweight tool call to verify connectivity.
The output presents results in a structured table with per-server PASSED/FAILED/PARTIAL outcomes.
